Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Complex The attackers use a multi-stage approach to disrupt the healthcare organization's operations, starting with reconnaissance, followed by resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and finally impact.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets File Windows Registry Application Log User Account Process Network Traffic DNS Record
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method File Modification (Mechanism) PowerShell Script Windows Registry Key Creation (Mechanism) Application Log Content (Mechanism) User Account Authentication (Mechanism) Reg.exe Utility Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism) File Access (Mechanism) Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker gathers information about the healthcare organization's network infrastructure by exploiting a resource leak.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attacker develops a custom malware similar to BlackEnergy3 to compromise the system.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker gains initial access using spear phishing emails with malicious attachments.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-108: Command Line Execution through SQL Injection
stage.description: The attacker executes the malware on compromised systems using PowerShell.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker schedules tasks to maintain persistence and re-establish connection.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker exploits vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to escalate privileges.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-191: Read Sensitive Constants Within an Executable
stage.description: The attacker obfuscates code to evade detection by security software.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker uses brute force attacks to obtain credentials.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker scans the network to identify potential targets.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attacker uses remote services to move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects sensitive data from local systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates data over alternative protocols to avoid detection.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts critical systems to cause maximum impact.

Scenario.summary: The attackers use a complex multi-stage approach to disrupt the healthcare organization's operations, starting with reconnaissance, followed by resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and finally impact.
